sections.name: Ozthoom
Ozthoom Guilds
sections.text: The most dangerous ozthooms are those who have rebelled against their masters. Ozthooms of this nature are more powerful, gathering others to form deadly “guilds” of assassins that pursue common goals in the most dangerous parts of the First World.

Ozthooms are shadowy killers who serve powerful fey creatures or even the Eldest—the demigods of the First World. Amid the courts of the Eldest or other powerful fey rulers, these sinister assassins lurk overhead as they await the call to action—implied threats akin to deadly weapons hung as decor in a royal hall. Ozthooms never speak aloud; when they feel the need to communicate at all, they do so in a telepathic whisper directly into their victim's mind. While an ozthoom's body is a strange, fleshy material, their deadly claws and cruel hooked wings are made from razor-sharp cold iron, a quality that makes them highly feared among other fey. A typical ozthoom is 10 feet tall and has an 8-foot wingspan, but it weighs less than 80 pounds.  
  
 While most ozthooms serve powerful masters, a few of these murderous fey have been left to their own devices and serve none but their own capricious whims. In some cases, their master has been slain, while in others, the ozthoom has been released from service for any number of reasons. An ozthoom left to indulge their cruel desires unrestrained is often the most dangerous ozthoom of them all.

traits.name: Fey
traits.text: Creatures of the First World are called the fey

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Naari
traits.text: Naaris are planar scions descended from efreet.

traits.name: Naari
traits.text: Naaris are planar scions descended from efreet.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: This weapon can be used to attack enemies up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent enemies. For creatures with reach, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

ability.effect: For each action spent to use this ability, the ozthoom creates one shadowy duplicate anywhere within 60 feet of themself. Shadow doubles have the same statistics as an ozthoom, but they have the summoned trait, 85 Hit Points, can't use Shadow Doubles or innate spells, and have an attack bonus of +25 for their Strikes. A shadow double that attempts a saving throw against a light effect can't get a result better than failure. Each double remains for 1 round, until it's reduced to 0 Hit Points, or until it moves further than 120 feet from the ozthoom, whichever comes first. Each round thereafter, the ozthoom can Sustain the effect to extend the duration of surviving duplicates by 1 round, to a maximum duration of 1 minute. The ozthoom can see through the eyes of all the shadow doubles at once. A character who Seeks can identify an ozthoom as real or a shadow double with a successful DC 39 Perception check.
ability.name: Shadow Doubles

traits.name: Illusion
traits.text: Effects and magic items with this trait involve false sensory stimuli.

traits.name: Occult
traits.text:
This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to occult magic.

traits.name: Shadow
traits.text:
Magic with this trait involves shadows or the energy of the Netherworld.  
  
 Planes with this trait are umbral with murky light. In the Netherworld, the radius of all light from light sources and the areas of light spells are halved.

ability.name: Sneak Attack
ability.text: A ozthoom's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to offguard creatures.

universal_monster_ability.name: Sneak Attack
universal_monster_ability.text: When the monster Strikes a creature that has the flat-footed condition with an agile or finesse melee weapon, an agile or finesse unarmed attack, or a ranged weapon attack, it also deals the listed precision damage. For a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, that weapon must also be an agile or finesse weapon.

universal_monster_ability.name: Lifesense
universal_monster_ability.text: Lifesense allows a monster to sense the vital essence of living and undead creatures within the listed range. The sense can distinguish between the vitality energy animating living creatures and the void energy animating undead creatures, much as sight distinguishes colors.

universal_monster_ability.name: Low-Light Vision
universal_monster_ability.text: The monster can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so it ignores the concealed condition due to dim light.

universal_monster_ability.name: Telepathy
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with telepathy can communicate mentally with any creatures within the listed radius, as long as they share a language. This doesn't give any special access to their thoughts and communicates no more information than normal speech would.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.
